real_questions.text: Walk us through your leverage on our metrics and against the regulatory asset coverage ratio — and the bridge between the two.

agency_intel.text: On BDC leverage, agencies take a dual approach: their own leverage metrics first, with the regulatory asset coverage ratio layered on top — similar to how they run their own bank capital evaluation against regulatory expectations. Know both numbers and the bridge.

For consumer/specialty lenders: the capital anchor depends on the model — off-balance-sheet-focused lenders are anchored on a leverage metric; on-balance-sheet lenders on tangible capital as the buffer against losses.

agency_intel: For CCPs and market infrastructure entities: regulatory capital provides a moat that supports higher ratings and strong credit protection — present the default-fund and capital waterfall framework affirmatively, as a credit strength.

agency_intel.text: S&P's BDC anchor: leverage — S&P is much more focused on leverage as the anchor to a strong rating.
